mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points over one month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal a tightening cycle that raises equity discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts (HOUST) during a recessionary regime signals weak consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings prospects decline.
failure_condition: HOUST declines are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or housing data fails to predict equity returns in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real sales (RSAFS) reflects weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenue expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or real sales data shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k new jobs signals labor market weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns through reduced consumer spending confidence.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or employment data fails to predict equity returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDP (GDPC1) and rise in housing starts (HOUST) indicates conflicting demand signals, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1-HOUST interaction term shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample tests.
